papers.title: Supercooled phase transitions in conformal dark sectors explain NANOGrav data
papers.brief_description: Classically conformal (scale-invariant) dark sector with U(1)' gauge symmetry, radiative Coleman-Weinberg symmetry breaking. Strong supercooled FOPT with breaking at the MeV scale produces a nanohertz SGWB; performs a fit to NANOGrav data including an SMBHB component. Provides an explicit best-fit benchmark.
papers.model_studied: Classically scale-invariant dark U(1)' with complex scalar (scalon) + dark gauge boson Z'; RG-improved one-loop CW effective potential with thermal + daisy resummation; lambda_sigma set negative by CW with dimensional transmutation. Higgs-portal coupling required tiny (|lambda_sigma_h| < 1e-10).
papers.similarity_to_target: Essentially the same model and the same target signal (nHz PTA GW from supercooled CW FOPT). Direct match for model structure, breaking mechanism, scale, and target band.
assumptions: Common temperature assumption / reheating after PT

papers.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector
papers.brief_description: Classically conformal dark U(1)' with dark Higgs (CW breaking) plus a dark fermion DM candidate. Supercooled FOPT at MeV scale produces nHz SGWB matching PTA; correlates GW with sub-GeV DM relic. Two explicit benchmark points (coupled and secluded).
papers.model_studied: Classically conformal hidden U(1)' with dark Higgs phi (m_A' = g v), CW radiative breaking, optional dark fermion with Yukawa y for DM; kinetic mixing to SM. One-loop + thermal + daisy effective potential.
papers.similarity_to_target: Same model class and same target signal. Includes a DM fermion not in the user's minimal model, but the scalar+gauge FOPT sector and target band match directly. Benchmark points directly usable as starting points (with the user's model having no DM Yukawa).
assumptions: Includes dark fermion DM (extra to user's minimal model)

papers.title: NANOGrav Signal from a Dark Conformal Phase Transition
papers.brief_description: nHz SGWB from a confining first-order transition of a nearly-conformal (strongly coupled) dark sector described by a dilaton/radion (5D holographic). Same target signal but different (strongly-coupled, confining) realization rather than weakly-coupled CW dark U(1).
papers.model_studied: Nearly-conformal dark Yang-Mills / holographic dilaton sector; supercooled confining FOPT.
papers.similarity_to_target: Same target signal (nHz PTA GW from supercooled conformal PT) but different model class (strongly-coupled dilaton, not perturbative CW U(1)). Useful for context on supercooled GW template and Neff/two-temperature treatment.
assumptions: Dark and visible sectors at different temperatures

papers.title: Gravitational waves from a dark U(1)_D phase transition in light of NANOGrav 12.5 yr data
papers.brief_description: Minimal dark U(1)_D with a complex singlet scalar + dark gauge boson, FOPT below the EW scale fitted to NANOGrav 12.5yr. Generic (non-conformal) scalar potential, so weaker supercooling than CW models.
papers.model_studied: Dark U(1)_D, complex scalar with explicit (tree-level) potential, dark vector boson; sub-EW-scale FOPT.
papers.similarity_to_target: Same gauge/field content and same PTA target, but explicit potential (not classically scale-invariant). Useful as a comparison/non-conformal counterpart; benchmark points not directly transferable to the CW case.

papers.title: Gravitational waves from scale-invariant vector dark matter model: Probing below the neutrino-floor
papers.brief_description: Scale-invariant SM extension with dark U(1)_D and vector DM (scalon). FOPT GW spectrum, but target band is mHz (eLISA/LISA), not nHz PTA.
papers.model_studied: Classically scale-invariant dark U(1)_D, scalon + vector DM, two free parameters, CW breaking.
papers.similarity_to_target: Very close model structure (scale-invariant dark U(1), CW), but GW signal targets the mHz LISA band, so benchmark points are at the wrong scale for PTA and not transferable.
assumptions: EW-to-TeV scale breaking -> mHz band (not PTA)

papers.title: Gauge-independent gravitational waves from a minimal dark U(1) sector with viable dark matter candidates
papers.brief_description: Minimal gauged dark U(1) (dark Higgs + dark photon, optional vectorlike fermions). Gauge-independent GW computation, scan over PTA and LISA bands; supercooled transitions give stronger signals.
papers.model_studied: Minimal dark U(1) with dark Higgs and dark photon, optional DM fermions; gauge-invariant treatment of the FOPT.
papers.similarity_to_target: Same field content and covers the PTA band; emphasizes gauge-independence (relevant methodological caveat). Not strictly classically scale-invariant per abstract; benchmarks not extracted (metadata only).

papers.title: On gauge dependence of gravitational waves from a first-order phase transition in classical scale-invariant U(1)' models
papers.brief_description: Methodological study of gauge dependence in GW predictions from classically scale-invariant U(1)' FOPTs; finds order-of-magnitude uncertainties from gauge artifacts in the resummed effective potential.
papers.model_studied: Classically scale-invariant U(1)' with CW breaking; focus on gauge-dependence of effective potential and resulting GW spectrum.
papers.similarity_to_target: Same model class; not a PTA-target benchmark paper but a crucial theory-uncertainty reference for the effective potential and FOPT parameter extraction (gauge dependence).
assumptions: Gauge-dependence quantified via R_xi gauge variation

novelty_notes: The exact model (classically scale-invariant / conformal dark U(1) with complex scalar + dark gauge boson, CW radiative breaking, supercooled FOPT, nHz GW matching PTA) has been studied directly in 2501.11619 and 2502.19478, both in 2025, including explicit best-fit benchmarks and NANOGrav fits. The model structure and target band are thus NOT novel. However, those works use their own pipelines; a dedicated, reproducible fopt-pta + PTArcade Bayesian inference campaign for the minimal (DM-free) version of this model, with transparent FOPT parameter extraction and gauge-uncertainty bookkeeping, remains a worthwhile and only partially-covered contribution.

conventions.note_on_user_model: drop dark-fermion Yukawa y for the user's minimal model

benchmark_hints.notes: Strong supercooling (alpha >> 1). Use as a starting point for PTArcade priors on g_D and v; the DM Yukawa is extra to the user's model and mildly affects the scalar mass.

conventions.note: very strong supercooling; large gap between T_n and T_p indicates significant reheating

benchmark_hints.notes: Extreme supercooling regime; useful for the strong-PT end of the prior.

Direct NANOGrav best-fit for the conformal dark U(1). 68% CL: g_D in [0.57,0.86], lambda_sigma in [-0.062,-0.028]. Strong guidance for g_D prior. radial(scalon) mass <-> beta_lambda v^2 in user's parametrization.

theory_uncertainties: Gauge dependence of the CW + thermal effective potential gives ~order-of-magnitude uncertainty on GW spectrum (1707.06765); recommend documenting gauge choice.
Supercooled transitions: large alpha and strong reheating require careful treatment of T_n vs T_p (percolation) and vacuum-domination; T_* choice strongly affects peak frequency.
Daisy/3D-EFT resummation choices affect supercooled bounce/beta-H (see broader literature, e.g. 3D EFT works).
Neff/BBN bounds on MeV-scale dark sector reheating constrain viable benchmarks.
recommended_next_steps: Hand 2502.19478 (Points A/B) and 2501.11619 (best-fit) to the fopt-agent as starting benchmarks for the minimal DM-free model.
Map the user's parametrization (v, g_D; m_A'^2 = g_D^2 v^2; m_rho^2 = (3 g_D^4/8pi^2) v^2) onto the literature benchmarks and check consistency of the radial-mode mass.
Set PTArcade priors on g_D near [0.55, 0.9] and v in the ~100-700 MeV range to land in the nHz band.
Flag gauge-dependence and percolation/T_* conventions as theory uncertainties for the critic and fopt agents.
